LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, JUL-19-2021 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT IDEO LP'S MOTION TO STAY ACTION, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STAY IS DENIED. THE COURT INSTEAD ORDERS ON ITS OWN MOTION THAT THIS ACTION BE CONSOLIDATED FOR PURPOSES OF TRIAL WITH MS. LYMAN'S EMPLOYMENT DISCRIMINATION AND WRONGFUL TERMINATION ACTION IN CASE NO. CGC-20-582985. THIS COURT'S CONSOLIDATION ORDER DOES NOT REOPEN DISCOVERY IN THAT ACTION, WHICH REMAINS CLOSED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR NO. 5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-27-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Action
Off Calendar Jul-19-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KELSEY K. CIARIMBOLI, Esq. of Bohm Law Group, Inc. (Tel: 866-920-1292) for plaintiff Alex Lyman;
Atty. NANCY L. MCCOY, Esq. of Simpson, Garrity, Innes & Jacuzzi (Tel: 925-322-8889) for defendant Ideo L.P.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT IDEO LP's Motion To Stay Action.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to stay is denied. The court instead orders on its own motion that this action be consolidated for purposes of trial with Ms. Lyman's employment discrimination and wrongful termination action in case no. CGC-20-582985. This Court's consolidation order does not reopen discovery in that action, which remains closed.

Defendant's request to stay this action is unsupported by the authorities cited, and is not in the interest of justice. "[P]rejudice inheres in cases like this one, where long delay inevitably results in dimmed memories and lost witnesses." (Lopez v. State of California (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 1292, 1295.)

Consolidation is the preferred course of action with derivative loss of consortium claims. "We suggest that hereafter the preferred method for asserting a claim of loss of consortium will be to join such a cause of action in the initial pleading with the other spouse's cause of action for negligent or intentional injury, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 378." (Rodriguez v. Bethlehem Steel Corp. (1974) 12 Cal.3d 382, 407, fn. 1.) This Court on its motion orders consolidation of the two actions for purposes of trial. (See Zakinov v. Ripple Labs, Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2019) 369 F.Supp.3d 950, 958 & fn. 6 [consolidation may be made on the court's own motion]; 4 B. Witkin, California Procedure (5th ed. 2020), Pleading 344 [same].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

Nancy L. McCoy, Esq. (925-322-8889 x 1440; NMcCoy@sgijlaw.com) of Simpson, Garrity, Innes & Jacuzzi PC for Defendant.
